content.text:
The error is:
```
GitHub Releases requires a tag
```

The issue: when triggered via `workflow_dispatch`, the tag is created and pushed (`git push origin "v${VERSION}"`) in a previous step, but `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` defaults its `tag_name` to `github.ref` which is `refs/heads/main` (not a tag ref). The action doesn't see a tag, so it fails.

The fix: explicitly pass `tag_name` to the release action so it uses the tag we just created.

- name: Create and push tag (workflow_dispatch only)
        if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
        run: |
          git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}" -m "Release ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
          git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"

      - name: Create arm64 DMG
        run: |
          ./scripts/create-dmg.sh --arch arm64 \
            --version ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }} \
            ${{ steps.signing.outputs.sign_args }}

      - name: Create x86_64 DMG

          ./scripts/create-dmg.sh --arch x86_64 \
